samples.turn1_fingerprint: episodic memory architecture with session summaries; inactivity timeout threshold in conversational AI; context window management via compressed session summaries; balancing token budget with conversational fidelity
samples.turn2_fingerprint: inactivity-triggered session collapse mechanism; variable timeout values (60 min morning, 30 min daytime, 120 min night); summary generation and memory extraction upon segment timeout; sentinel markers and session boundary management
samples.turn2_raw:
<analysis>       
<entities>
None
</entities>  
<relevant_passages>
<passage id="mem_969d3faf" still_relevant="true">Abandoned aggressive topic-based summarization in favor of inactivity-triggered compression because topics blend together and circle back in natural conversation, making binary topic detection unreliable; accumulating conversation within context window preserves connective tissue of references that return to earlier points</passage>
<passage id="mem_a17581ea" still_relevant="true">Context window architecture is intentionally CURATED, LIVE, and DYNAMIC - manifest provides conversation topology, summaries compress relevant context, targeted memory surfacing presents right information at right time to maintain genuine continuity rather than fleeting AI interactions</passage>
<passage id="mem_c052e7dc" still_relevant="true">Replaced ProgressiveHotCache with new session management system: uses inactivity-triggered collapses with sentinels marking chat boundaries, timeouts (60 min morning / 30 min daytime / 120 min night), summary generation, message removal, and memory extraction; on next session load, three prior session summaries are loaded into context window with session begin/end markers</passage>
<passage id="mem_82dbc554" still_relevant="true">Improved session continuity in MIRA context window by preserving final message pairs from collapsed segment before session boundary marker; creates gradient from compressed narrative summaries through visible recent exchanges to current session, approximating human rolling buffer perception better than hard cutoff</passage>
<passage id="mem_d4b82821" still_relevant="true">MIRA system uses timeout-based session collapse: when a conversation segment times out (no new messages for X time), fires event that removes segment from context, generates summary, extracts memories, and inserts summary at sentinel position in context window</passage>
</relevant_passages>         
<query_expansion>
inactivity-triggered session collapse mechanism; variable timeout values (60 min morning, 30 min daytime, 120 min night); summary generation and memory extraction upon segment timeout; sentinel markers and session boundary management
</query_expansion> 
</analysis>

samples.current_message: Man, I tell you what... I want to start playing OSRS again but life as a business owner and adult is just so tiring and I'm stretched thin. I have no time for playing video games because I'll play for 15 minutes and turn it off because I get bored or frustrated. It isn't even an attention span thing... I just don't have a desire to play it. I had almost 2000 hours in tanks in Battlefield 3 and now I've got probably 20 hours into Battlefield 6. Idk. I know this happens to a lot of adults but it isn't fun.
